title: Phyllorkis petiolaris
intro: Phyllorkis petiolaris, also known as the "Finger Orchid", is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it potentially useful in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and inflammatory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis petiolaris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Phyllorkis petiolaris is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, although its use is largely limited to folklore and has not been extensively studied or scientifically validated.
taxonomy: Phyllorkis petiolaris is classified in the family Orchidaceae. This family includes over 30,000 species of orchids, which are one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. Phyllorkis petiolaris is further classified in the subfamily Orchidoideae, which contains terrestrial and epiphytic orchids. Within the subfamily Orchidoideae, Phyllorkis petiolaris belongs to the tribe Coelogyneae, a group of terrestrial orchids. The exact classification of Phyllorkis petiolaris is not well-represented in available data.
